item.title: EXPLOSION HEARD NEAR U.S. EMBASSY IN BAGHDAD, SIRENS SOUND - REUTERS WITNESS
item.description: EXPLOSION HEARD NEAR U.S. EMBASSY IN BAGHDAD, SIRENS SOUND - REUTERS WITNESS

item.title:
IRAN’S SUPREME LEADER SURVIVED STRIKE BY MINUTES
Mojtaba Khamenei narrowly survived U.S. and Israeli missile strikes that hit his residence in Tehran after stepping outside moments before the attack, according to leaked audio reported by The Telegraph.
The strikes — which killed his father Ali Khamenei and several senior Iranian officials — hit the compound on Feb. 28 with multiple missiles. Mojtaba Khamenei was wounded in the leg, while his wife and son were killed.
The recording, attributed to an official in the former leader’s office, says the attack targeted several locations in the complex and appeared aimed at eliminating the entire leadership.
item.description:
IRAN’S SUPREME LEADER SURVIVED STRIKE BY MINUTES

Mojtaba Khamenei narrowly survived U.S. and Israeli missile strikes that hit his residence in Tehran after stepping outside moments before the attack, according to leaked audio reported by The Telegraph.

The strikes — which killed his father Ali Khamenei and several senior Iranian officials — hit the compound on Feb. 28 with multiple missiles. Mojtaba Khamenei was wounded in the leg, while his wife and son were killed.

The recording, attributed to an official in the former leader’s office, says the attack targeted several locations in the complex and appeared aimed at eliminating the entire leadership.

item.title: - CUBA'S NATIONAL ELECTRIC GRID COLLAPSES, SAYS CUBA'S NATIONAL ELECTRIC UNION
item.description: - CUBA'S NATIONAL ELECTRIC GRID COLLAPSES, SAYS CUBA'S NATIONAL ELECTRIC UNION

item.title:
TRUMP SAYS IRAN OIL HUB STRIKE STILL AN OPTION
Donald Trump said the U.S. could still target oil facilities on Kharg Island, Iran’s main crude export hub, despite earlier sparing its energy infrastructure.
The U.S. previously struck military targets on the island but avoided oil facilities. Trump warned that decision could change if Iran disrupts shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
Kharg Island handles about 90% of Iran’s oil exports, mostly to China, meaning any attack on its infrastructure could quickly push global oil prices higher and intensify inflation pressures.
Trump also said U.S. forces destroyed Iranian mine-laying ships to help reopen the strategic waterway, through which about one-fifth of global energy supply passes.
item.description:
TRUMP SAYS IRAN OIL HUB STRIKE STILL AN OPTION

Donald Trump said the U.S. could still target oil facilities on Kharg Island, Iran’s main crude export hub, despite earlier sparing its energy infrastructure.

The U.S. previously struck military targets on the island but avoided oil facilities. Trump warned that decision could change if Iran disrupts shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

Kharg Island handles about 90% of Iran’s oil exports, mostly to China, meaning any attack on its infrastructure could quickly push global oil prices higher and intensify inflation pressures.

Trump also said U.S. forces destroyed Iranian mine-laying ships to help reopen the strategic waterway, through which about one-fifth of global energy supply passes.
